title: Did you follow the local news this week? Take our Greater Boston news quiz.
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Boston.com offers a local news quiz to test readers' knowledge of Greater Boston stories.
Peter Chianca has been Boston.com's general assignment editor since 2019.
The news quiz covers trending stories and aims to keep participants informed about Greater Boston events.

title: Citywide needle pickup requests fluctuated from 2020 to 2025, but surged in these neighborhoods
author: Sana Muneer
bullets: Needle pickup requests in Boston surged 71% from 2022 to 2025 despite a 17% decrease since 2020.
Jamaica Plain saw a 149% increase in needle reports from 235 in 2020 to 585 in 2025.
Back Bay had a 73% rise in pickup requests, while Fenway-Kenmore experienced a 54% decline over five years.

title: Kaity Cimo is running the Boston Marathon for the Alzheimer’s Association with her mother in mind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Kaity Cimo will run the Boston Marathon to honor her mother and support the Alzheimer's Association.
Cimo is motivated by her mother's journey with Alzheimer's, particularly their shared memories in the kitchen.
She draws inspiration from her mother's nurturing through cooking, representing love and care.

title: On eve of trade deadline, Bruins make a horrid case for becoming buyers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins, 11-14-4 on the road, face uncertainty entering the NHL trade deadline after losing to Nashville.
General Manager Don Sweeney considers roster upgrades but remains cautious about trading valuable picks or prospects.
Boston holds the final Eastern Conference Wild Card spot, challenged by Columbus closely trailing in the standings.

title: Judge pauses deportation of Boston area Irish man held in custody for 6 months
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Judge pauses the deportation of Seamus Culleton, an Irish man in Texas detention, pending review of his case.
Culleton overstayed a visa in 2009; he criticized conditions at East Montana ICE Detention Camp in media.
Culleton's arrest occurred at a Home Depot in Saugus; he has a pending green card application and no U.S. criminal record.

title: Victim in early morning Foxborough fire identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: George Schubert, 81, was identified as the victim of a fatal house fire in Foxborough on Granite Street.
The fire occurred around 3 a.m., also killing Schubert's dog; the cause is under investigation.
Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville fire departments collaborated to extinguish the fire within an hour.

title: Boston tree canopy has expanded, in step with plans for a cleaner, greener city
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's tree canopy expanded by 151 acres from 2019 to 2024, increasing city coverage to 28.5 percent.
Public parks and rights-of-way primarily contributed to the increase, unlike the previous five-year period with no net change.
East Boston, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, and West Roxbury saw significant canopy gains; Hyde Park and Mattapan experienced losses.

title: Kraft agrees to help cover World Cup security costs, but Foxbourough leaders still unsatisfied
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Robert Kraft's group commits to assisting with $7.8 million in World Cup security costs at Gillette Stadium.
Foxborough officials unsatisfied with Kraft Group and BS26 assurances for covering World Cup public safety expenses.
Foxborough Select Board to decide on World Cup event license during March 17 meeting, citing financial concerns.

title: Two Saints Tavern, a familiar bar for Northeastern students, is closing after 10 years in Fenway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Saints Tavern in Fenway is closing after nearly a decade, partly due to rising insurance costs.
The bar, popular with Northeastern students, moved under current ownership in 2017, featuring a partnership with Ken Casey.
Insurance costs rose dramatically, with quotes jumping from $18,000 to between $75,000 and $82,000 annually.

title: 4 questions facing Celtics ahead of Jayson Tatum’s expected return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum is expected to return for the Celtics against the Mavericks on Friday after recovering from a ruptured Achilles.
The Celtics are currently second in the Eastern Conference with a 41-21 record, aiming for a strong playoff push with Tatum's return.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla faces decisions on managing Tatum’s minutes and adjusting the starting lineup with his reintroduction.
